key_highlights: U.S. added 172,000 jobs in May, unemployment steady at 4.3%, fueling Fed hawkishness and triggering a $1.4 trillion S&P 500 sell-off.
ECB is set to hike rates, joining global tightening cycle; ECB steps up as G7's lead hawk.
SpaceX IPO looms with a $1.77 trillion valuation target, but warnings of retail investor 'fleecing' and tokenized access via Bybit.
Crude inventories perilously low, but OPEC+ agreed a symbolic quota increase; airline chiefs grapple with fuel shock.
Bitcoin drops below $60,000, worst performance in a decade, as AI stocks attract investors.
75% of U.S. homes unaffordable; Dave Ramsey calls it the most unrealistic market in 100 years.
AI mega stock deals raise specter of more shares than buyers; OpenAI readies 'superapp' pivot ahead of planned IPO.
U.S.-China rivalry laid bare by Argentine river contract; GE sees potential for more China deals after Trump-Xi meeting.
South Korea unveils measures to stem won slide; Israel's central bank intervenes to curb shekel.
Chips sector bloodbath drags Nasdaq down 4.2%; S&P 500 to add Marvell and Flex.

sectors.trend: Sharp sell-off: Nasdaq down 4.2% in a chips sector bloodbath. AI mega stock deals raise concerns about oversupply of shares. SpaceX IPO hype vs. warnings of retail investor fleecing. OpenAI pivots to 'superapp' ahead of IPO.
sectors.implications: Investor rotation out of high-growth tech into value/defensive. AI bubble fears resurface. SpaceX IPO could be a major liquidity event but risks significant downside for retail. Semiconductor weakness may persist on demand concerns.

sectors.trend: Crude inventories perilously low but OPEC+ symbolic quota increase. Gasoline prices dipping 17 cents nationally. Airline chiefs grapple with fuel shock. Iran war impact debated at OPEC+ and aviation conferences. Petronas seeks Turkmenistan gas access.
sectors.implications: Energy sector remains volatile with geopolitical risk premium. Low inventories support prices, but demand destruction fears (airlines, consumers) cap upside. Renewables and alternative energy may see increased interest.

sectors.trend: ECB rate hike primed, Fed hawkish after strong jobs report. Mortgage rates rising, 75% of homes unaffordable. 74% of buyers would consider 50-year mortgage. CD and money market rates around 4% APY. HELOC rates rising.
sectors.implications: Higher-for-longer rate environment pressures housing and consumer credit. Banks benefit from wider net interest margins but face credit risk. Housing market affordability crisis deepens. Savers benefit from high yields.

sectors.trend: U.S.-China rivalry intensifies (Argentine river contract). Iran war continues, no peace deal in sight. U.S. offers to work with Iran on uranium. South Korea and Israel intervene in FX. German carmakers weigh China/defense tie-ups.
sectors.implications: Geopolitical uncertainty remains high, supporting defense stocks and commodities. FX volatility in EM and developed markets. Supply chain diversification continues. Defense and infrastructure spending likely to increase.

sectors.trend: Companies cashing in on consumers' unwillingness to bargain shop. Airline chiefs grapple with fuel shock. Target recalls baby wipes. 75% of homes unaffordable. Dave Ramsey calls market unrealistic.
sectors.implications: Consumer resilience is tested by inflation and high rates. Bargain shopping decline may indicate brand loyalty or fatigue. Travel demand remains strong but fuel costs pressure airlines. Housing-related spending likely to slow.

sectors.trend: Bitcoin drops below $60,000, worst performance in a decade. AI stocks attract investors away from crypto. Bybit to open SpaceX tokenized IPO access.
sectors.implications: Crypto market underperformance vs. AI/tech. Regulatory and macro headwinds persist. Tokenized IPOs could bridge traditional and crypto markets but add complexity.

key_themes: Global monetary tightening (Fed, ECB, others) weighing on risk assets.
AI bubble debate: mega deals, IPO hype, and retail investor risk.
Geopolitical fragmentation: U.S.-China rivalry, Iran conflict, FX interventions.
Housing affordability crisis and creative mortgage solutions.
Energy security vs. transition: low inventories, OPEC+ moves, airline fuel shock.
Space race: SpaceX IPO, Caribbean spaceport, NASA supersonic jet.
Consumer resilience under pressure: inflation, rates, and spending shifts.
Crypto winter deepens as AI steals the spotlight.

risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce duration in bond portfolios; favor floating-rate instruments; increase cash allocation; hedge with put options on tech ETFs.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: AI/tech bubble burst (SpaceX IPO, mega deals, chips sell-off)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid chasing IPO hype; take profits on overvalued AI names; diversify into value/defensive sectors; use stop-losses on high-beta tech.
Increase exposure to defense, energy, and commodities; hedge with gold and VIX calls; reduce exposure to EM equities and currencies.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Housing market crash / consumer credit stress
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid homebuilder and consumer finance stocks; favor mortgage servicers; consider short positions on REITs with high leverage.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Energy supply shock (low inventories, Iran war, OPEC+ under-delivery)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Maintain overweight in energy equities and commodities; use call options on oil; invest in renewable energy as a hedge.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Hedge fund crowding and systemic risk
risk_assessment.mitigation: Monitor prime brokerage data; reduce exposure to crowded trades (e.g., AI mega-caps); increase liquidity buffers.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy energy stocks on pullbacks (e.g., Chevron, Woodside Energy, SLB)
opportunities.rationale: Low inventories, geopolitical risk premium, and OPEC+ discipline support oil prices. Airline fuel shock indicates demand remains strong. Valuations attractive after recent sell-off.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate high-quality value stocks with strong cash flows (e.g., industrial titans under $40 benefiting from tariff supercycle)
opportunities.rationale: Tariff supercycle and infrastructure spending boost domestic industrials. Low valuation provides margin of safety in a hawkish rate environment.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in defense and infrastructure (e.g., AECOM, GE)
opportunities.rationale: Geopolitical tensions and U.S.-China rivalry drive defense spending. GE sees potential for more China deals. Infrastructure expansion supported by policy.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy high-yield savings and CDs at 4%+ APY
opportunities.rationale: Rising rates make cash equivalents attractive. Lock in yields before potential rate cuts later. Low risk, steady income.

opportunities.recommendation: Selectively buy beaten-down tech with strong fundamentals (e.g., Dell, Marvell, Flex)
opportunities.rationale: Chips sell-off creates entry points. S&P 500 addition for Marvell and Flex signals quality. Dell has strong enterprise demand. Use limit orders.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to high-beta tech and AI hype names
defensive_moves.rationale: Nasdaq down 4.2%, chips bloodbath, AI mega deals raise oversupply fears. SpaceX IPO could be a 'fleecing'. Take profits and rotate to defensives.
Fed and ECB hiking, rate uncertainty high. Cash provides optionality to buy dips. Short-duration bonds reduce interest rate risk.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against geopolitical risk with gold and VIX calls
defensive_moves.rationale: Iran war, U.S.-China rivalry, FX interventions. Gold is a safe haven. VIX calls protect against tail risk from escalation.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid homebuilder and consumer finance stocks
defensive_moves.rationale: 75% of homes unaffordable, mortgage rates rising, 50-year mortgages considered. Consumer credit stress likely. Avoid until housing market stabilizes.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to EM equities and currencies
defensive_moves.rationale: South Korea and Israel intervening in FX, EM long bonds missing out on peace dividend. Strong USD and geopolitical risks hurt EM.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Bearish bias due to hawkish central banks (Fed, ECB), geopolitical tensions (Iran, U.S.-China), and AI/tech sell-off. S&P 500 could test support levels near 5,000. Volatility likely elevated. Defensive sectors (energy, healthcare, utilities) may outperform. Bitcoin may continue to slide. Housing market remains under pressure.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Cautiously optimistic if rate hikes pause and geopolitical tensions de-escalate. AI and infrastructure spending are long-term growth drivers. Energy transition and defense spending provide structural support. Housing affordability may force policy changes. SpaceX IPO could be a major event but requires careful timing.
key_catalysts: Fed and ECB rate decisions in June/July 2026.
Iran peace deal progress or escalation.
SpaceX IPO pricing and retail demand.
U.S.-China trade deal implementation after Trump-Xi meeting.
Q2 earnings season (especially tech and energy).
AI regulation and public ownership debates.
watch_list: WTI crude oil price (support at $70, resistance at $85).
SpaceX IPO news and retail participation.
U.S. CPI data for May (due June 12).
